questions.promptTex: \text{Find the Simple Interest (SI) on a Principal of } \$5000 \text{ at a rate of } 8\% \text{ per annum} \\ \text{for } 3 \text{ years. Answer in dollars.}
hints: **Simple Interest formula:** $SI = \dfrac{P \cdot R \cdot T}{100}$, where $P$ = Principal, $R$ = Rate %, $T$ = Time in years.

questions.promptTex: \text{Principal (P) = } \$6000, \text{ Rate (R) = } 10\% \text{ per annum, Time (T) = } 4 \text{ years.} \\ \text{What is the total Amount (A) after Simple Interest? (in dollars)}
hints: **First find SI**, then add it to the Principal to get the Amount.

questions.promptTex: \text{What Principal (P) earns } \$720 \text{ as Simple Interest at a rate of } 6\% \text{ per annum} \\ \text{over } 4 \text{ years? Answer in dollars.}
hints: **Rearrange** $SI = PRT/100$ to solve for $P$: $P = \dfrac{100 \cdot SI}{R \cdot T}$.

steps.text: **Start from the SI formula and isolate $P$:**

steps.math: SI = \dfrac{P \cdot R \cdot T}{100} \;\Longrightarrow\; P = \dfrac{100 \cdot SI}{R \cdot T}

questions.promptTex: \text{Find the Compound Interest (CI) on } \$10{,}000 \text{ at } 10\% \text{ per annum} \\ \text{for } 2 \text{ years, compounded annually. Answer in dollars.}
hints: **Compound Interest formula:** $A = P(1 + R/100)^T$, then $CI = A - P$.

questions.promptTex: \text{For Principal = } \$5000, \text{ Rate = } 10\%, \text{ Time = } 2 \text{ years,} \\ \text{find the difference: Compound Interest } - \text{ Simple Interest. (in dollars)}
hints: Why? CI in year 2 earns extra interest on the year-1 SI. That extra equals $R\%$ of year-1's $SI = P \cdot (R/100)^2$ for a 2-year period.

questions.promptTex: \text{Find the Amount (A) for Principal } \$16{,}000 \text{ at } 12.5\% \text{ per annum,} \\ \text{compounded annually, after } 2 \text{ years. Answer in dollars.}
\text{Find the CI on } \$4000 \text{ at } 10\% \text{ per annum compounded half-yearly} \\ \text{for } 1 \text{ year. Answer in dollars.}
hints: **Half-yearly compounding:** halve the rate, double the periods. New rate = 5%, new periods = 2.

steps.text: **For half-yearly compounding**: rate per period = $10\%/2 = 5\%$. Number of periods in 1 year: $2$.

questions.promptTex: \text{Using the Rule of 72: at what compound rate (\%) does money DOUBLE in } 6 \text{ years?} \\ \text{(Round to nearest integer.)}
hints: **Rule of 72 (mental shortcut for CI):** Doubling time $T \approx 72/R$, so $R \approx 72/T$.

steps.text: **Rule of 72** (a famous mental approximation for compound growth): doubling time satisfies $T \cdot R \approx 72$.

questions.promptTex: \text{A Principal of } \$5000 \text{ grows to } \$6050 \text{ in } 2 \text{ years under compound interest.} \\ \text{Find the annual rate of interest } R \text{ in percent.}

steps.text: **Set up the CI equation and isolate the growth factor:**

questions.promptTex: \text{Find the SI on } \$3000 \text{ at } 6\% \text{ per annum for } 5 \text{ years. Answer in dollars.}
\text{Money doubles in } 8 \text{ years under Simple Interest.} \\ \text{Find the rate of interest (in \%).}
hints: **Doubling means** the total Amount = $2P$, so the SI earned = $P$ (one extra principal worth).
Set up: $P = (P \cdot R \cdot 8)/100$, then cancel $P$ and solve for $R$.

steps.text: **Doubling means $SI = P$** (you earn one full Principal worth as interest).

questions.promptTex: \text{Find the CI on } \$8000 \text{ at } 5\% \text{ per annum compounded \emph{quarterly}} \\ \text{for } 1 \text{ year. Answer in dollars (round to 2 decimals).}
hints: **Quarterly compounding:** divide rate by 4, multiply periods by 4. Rate per quarter = $5\%/4 = 1.25\%$. Periods = 4.

steps.text: **Quarterly compounding:** rate per quarter $= 5\%/4 = 1.25\%$. Periods in 1 year $= 4$.

questions.promptTex: \text{At what annual rate of Simple Interest will } \$500 \\ \text{grow to } \$575 \text{ in } 5 \text{ years? Answer in percent.}
\text{Investment of } \$20{,}000 \text{ at } 10\% \text{ per annum compound interest.} \\ \text{Find the Amount after } 3 \text{ years (in dollars).}
\text{If SI for } 3 \text{ years on a given Principal and Rate is } \$1800, \\ \text{what is the SI for } 5 \text{ years on the same Principal and Rate? Answer in dollars.}
hints: **Key idea:** Simple Interest is directly proportional to Time (for fixed $P$ and $R$).
